title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds atomic feed ingestion unit of work from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of atomic feed ingestion unit of work while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.
Commit feed metadata, entries, revisions, validators, next schedule, and audit record together or preserve the prior admitted version.
Checkpoint only replay-safe boundaries and bind each checkpoint to source, validator, parser version, schema, and attempt identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Ingestion State Machine
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 5963 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Ingestion State Machine.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Ingestion State Machine, keep Feed Go Ingestion State Machine connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-ingestion-reconciliation-w1nvqi" that uses case 5439 to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects.
